episode_summary: The gang discuss a Tory conference stall featuring a shooting simulator and other party-conference oddities, the Rosetta mission’s findings including a comet’s cat-dolphin-like hum, the rituals and mishaps of Nobel Prize ceremonies and after-parties, and the recovery of stolen Van Gogh paintings—framed via their cocaine-equivalent value. Listener-submitted news snippets add extra bizarre headlines along the way.

facts.fact: My fact this week is that one of these stalls at the Conservative Party conference this week is a grounds shooting simulator.
My fact is that comets sound like a cross between a cat and a dolphin.
OK, my fact this week is that Nobel Prize winners always immediately return their award so they don't lose it in the subsequent party.

My fact this week is that two recently recovered stolen vangoff paintings would buy you enough cocaine that you could snort a continuous line from here to Moscow. And you'd still have a bit left over for when you get there.
